explanation: This command matches the Sigma rule by using curl.exe with the -O flag and a URL containing an IP address, ending with a common executable extension (.exe).

This command uses the long-form --output flag instead of -O, which is an equivalent substitution. It still matches the Sigma rule by downloading from an IP address and using a common executable extension.
This command adds a trailing space after the filename, which is a safe mutation via insertion. It still matches the Sigma rule.
This command adds a trailing quote, which is a safe mutation via insertion. It still matches the Sigma rule.
This command adds an optional --create-dirs flag, which is a safe mutation via insertion. It still matches the Sigma rule.
This command adds an optional --silent flag, which is a safe mutation via insertion. It still matches the Sigma rule.
This command explicitly specifies the output filename using --output, which is a safe mutation via substitution. It still matches the Sigma rule.
This command uses --remote-name instead of -O, which is an equivalent substitution. It still matches the Sigma rule.
This command adds an explicit output filename using --output, which is a safe mutation via insertion. It still matches the Sigma rule.
This command adds multiple optional flags, which is a safe mutation via insertion. It still matches the Sigma rule.
